qc_feedback: - Non-Failing Issus: • [Non-Fail - Tool/MCP Server Mentioned] The prompt references an MCP server by name : "Jordan told me she has been sending us referrals and some of them might be sitting in the CRM untouched.". • [Non-Fail - Up to 15% Moderate Errors] ◦ [Major Issues] [Criteria Not Self-Contained] [C#17] C#17 evaluates if "the final response identifies which of Derek's closed borrowers he has not contacted since closing". The criterion doesn't list the expected names of the borrowers. In order to verify whether the model flagged the right ones extra information would be needed, and the Criterion cannot be evaluated against the model response without access to the universe, personas, tool schemas, and/or external facts/information. ◦ [Moderate Issues] [Overly Broad Criteria] [C#1] The prompt asks to "Figure out what she [Alicia] has sent us, whether any of those people are still waiting to hear back, and reach out to anyone who has got cold.". As per OE, those should be Kimberly Hayes, Sanjay Brennan, as Kimberly Hayes is excluded. The criterion specifies only that the mail should be sent to "at least one Alicia Grant referral lead whose CRM status is "new" or "contacted", but this constrain is too broad. - Discussion: • [Prompt] The prompt is from Derek Moss's perspective. • [Runs] Fewer than 3 runs were not successfully completed. 0 runs pass.
